rationale: My round-1 Finding A (spec.md still described the deleted `refutation_discharged` discharge path) is ADDRESSED. Verified in an absolute /tmp clone at pinned 2b4fb8f0: `git diff 41d8e8af 2b4fb8f0` touches ONLY spec.md (+12), plan.md (+4/-2), and the round-1 review artifacts — zero .go files, no stray file, exactly the scoped doc-only delta the BRIEF claims. spec.md's new '## Amendment (2026-07-10, implementation)' is a pure append below the existing approved text (diff shows zero '-' lines removing original prose) — forward-only (ADR-0023) compliant. It explicitly names and supersedes every stale reference (§7 ADR-Touchpoints, R1/R2 trichotomy, In Scope re-tally line, AC11(d)/(e)/(f), R2 falsifiers), and its claims check out against the shipped code: grep for 'discharge' in internal/cmd finds only explanatory comments (2 files), zero implementation; the three AC11 test names it cites (TestPanelRefuted_SatisfyFromMarker_PanelGone, _MarkerCarriesReasonEvidence, _NaturalResolution_NoObligation) all exist verbatim in internal/complete/panel_refuted_test.go. Tracker hygiene (my round-1 low-sev note): mvp8.3 is now CLOSED, epic mvp8 remains OPEN — as expected, resolves at impl-approve; not a blocker. Finding B: weighed the disposition — bd show confirms mindspec-o4fd exists as a genuine P1 OPEN bead matching the scope note's text exactly (jsonl export in the /tmp clone simply lags the live bd DB, not an over-claim). Out-of-scope-follow-up + honest scope note is the right call given R1/R2's explicit complete-scoping and O1/F1's clean in-mechanism confirmation. Re-ran build/gofmt/vet/tests at 2b4fb8f0 in /tmp: all green (no code changed, as expected). Clean git status, no stray artifacts.

findings.description: spec.md's discharge-path language (AC11(d)/(e)/(f), R1/R2 trichotomy, §7 ADR-Touchpoints, In Scope re-tally line) reconciled via a dated, forward-only-compliant superseding addendum that accurately matches the shipped no-discharge design (verified test names + zero-Discharge-implementation grep).
.beads/issues.jsonl mvp8.3/epic mvp8 in_progress/open loose end — mvp8.3 now CLOSED; epic mvp8 remains OPEN pending impl-approve auto-close, as expected. Not a blocker.

Weighed Finding B (impl-approve/FinalizeEpic raw-bd-close bypass) disposition as out-of-scope tracked follow-up. mindspec-o4fd (P1, OPEN) confirmed genuinely filed via bd show, matching the spec.md scope note's text. Disposition is reasonable: R1/R2 explicitly scoped to `complete`; O1/F1 confirmed no in-mechanism gap; scope note is honest and non-over-claiming.
